prompt_text: Is Supermetrics better than Fivetran for automate marketing reporting across all channels?
response:
Supermetrics is usually **better for automating multi‑channel marketing reporting** (especially into Sheets/Excel/Looker Studio and simple dashboards), while Fivetran is **better if you have a data warehouse and want broader, company‑wide data integration** across marketing plus other domains.[1][4][5][6]

To choose between them for “automated marketing reporting across all channels,” focus on three things: who will use it, where the data will live, and how advanced your stack is.

---

### 1. Fit for marketing reporting use case

**Supermetrics – optimized for marketers & reporting**

- Purpose‑built for **marketing and sales data**; connectors and schemas are tuned for ad platforms, analytics, CRM, etc.[1][5]  
- Designed so **marketers can self‑serve** without heavy engineering support.[1][4][5]  
- Pushes data directly into **Google Sheets, Excel, Looker Studio, and BI tools**, which is exactly where many teams build reports.[1][4][6][7]  
- Good coverage of **100–120+ marketing connectors** (ads, analytics, SEO, CRM), often with deep access to fields.[1][5][7]

**Fivetran – broader data integration platform**

- Built as an **enterprise data pipeline** tool: marketing, product, finance, ops, databases, etc., all into a warehouse.[2][5]  
- Best suited when you want **Customer 360** and cross‑department analytics, not just marketing.[2][5]  
- Destinations are mostly **cloud data warehouses and lakes** (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks, etc.), not spreadsheets.[1][2][4]  
- Has **150+ connectors** across many categories, but it is not marketing‑specialized.[2][5][6]

For *marketing reporting specifically*, most independent comparisons say **Supermetrics is the more natural choice** if you don’t have a mature data engineering setup.[4][5][6]

---

### 2. Destinations and workflow

| Need | Supermetrics | Fivetran |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Direct to Sheets/Excel | Native, strong[1][4][6][7] | Not supported; everything goes via a warehouse[2][6] |
| Direct to Looker Studio & similar BI | Native connectors & templates[1][4][6][7] | Via warehouse then BI; no “direct to dashboard”[2][4][5] |
| Direct to warehouse/lake | Supports BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, S3, etc., but with lighter transformation & ops[1][5] | Core focus; highly automated, “set‑and‑forget” pipelines[2][5] |

If your “automated marketing reporting across all channels” lives in **Sheets/Excel or Looker Studio**, Supermetrics is clearly better suited.[1][4][6][7]  
If your reporting is built on top of a **central warehouse (dbt, BI on top)**, Fivetran becomes more compelling.[2][4][5]

---

### 3. Team skills and ownership

- **Supermetrics** is recommended when:
  - The main users are **marketers/analysts without strong SQL or engineering**.[1][4][5]  
  - You want **scheduled refreshes** (hourly/daily) and don’t need near‑real‑time.[4]  
  - You’re okay building reports in Sheets/BI tools and doing light transformations there.[4][5]

- **Fivetran** is recommended when:
  - You have **data engineers or analysts with SQL** and a **data warehouse** in place or planned.[2][4][5]  
  - You want **centralized, historical, and highly reliable pipelines**, often for many business domains beyond marketing.[2][4][5]  
  - You’re building a **modern data stack** (warehouse + dbt + BI) and marketing is just one input.[2][5]

So if this is led by the **marketing team with little engineering support**, Supermetrics is usually a better practical fit.[1][4][5]

---

### 4. Connectors and “all channels”

- Supermetrics: **100–120+ marketing/sales connectors**, focused on ad platforms, analytics, SEO, CRM.[1][5][7]  
- Fivetran: **150+ connectors** across marketing, apps, databases, and files.[2][5][6]

If “all channels” means **a wide range of marketing platforms** (Meta, Google, LinkedIn, TikTok, etc.), both can handle this; Supermetrics often provides **deeper marketing‑specific fields**, while Fivetran gives you better **breadth across non‑marketing systems**.[1
